task_completion.description: The agent loaded the image sequence into AstroImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ main window or 'Stack' window showing the loaded images (e.g., slice 1/186, filename matching WASP-12 field).

task_completion.description: The agent placed apertures on at least 5 stars (including comparison stars) using the Multi-Aperture tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image display window showing colored aperture rings (usually green for target, red for comparison) on at least 5 distinct stars.

task_completion.description: The agent generated and displayed light curves for the selected stars.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Multi-plot Main' window or similar plotting interface showing graphs of Relative Flux vs. Time/Frame. One graph should likely show a dip.

task_completion.description: The agent created the text report file 'variable_star_report.txt'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file listing showing 'variable_star_report.txt' or a text editor/terminal displaying the file.

task_completion.description: The report correctly identifies the variable star (e.g., by aperture label like 'T1' or 'Star 1', or pixel coordinates).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text content in 'variable_star_report.txt' identifying a specific star as the variable one. The identified star should correspond to the one showing a dip in the plots.

task_completion.description: The report states the approximate transit depth (~1.4%) and timing of the minimum.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text content in 'variable_star_report.txt' stating a depth around 1.4% (or 0.014) and a specific frame number or JD for the minimum brightness.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the AstroImageJ GUI for photometry as requested, rather than external scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the AstroImageJ interface (aperture rings, plot windows, dialogs) being used.

integrity.description: Verify the agent derived the values from the data rather than looking up 'WASP-12 transit depth' online.
integrity.visual_evidence: The reported depth and timing should be consistent with the light curve visible in the screenshots (e.g., if the plot shows a noisy 1.5% dip, the report shouldn't claim a precise 1.38% literature value).

integrity.description: Verify the report content matches the visual analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The star identified in the report (e.g., 'T1') should be the one showing the dip in the 'Multi-plot' window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target variable star is WASP-12. The expected transit depth is approximately 1.4% (0.014). The dataset contains 186 frames. The correct light curve will show a distinct periodic dip (transit) compared to the flat comparison stars.
